conditions: Heart disease (history of myocardial infarction with coronary stent, 2019)
medications: Nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL (as needed for chest pain)
Omeprazole 20 mg (daily, for reflux/aspirin stomach protection)
Diclofenac 1% topical gel (as needed for joint pain)
health.mental_health: Ongoing financial stress after bankruptcy contributes to anxiety and sleep disruption; he has been using brief telehealth counseling through his employer EAP to manage worry and concentration issues.
recent_visits: Ophthalmology follow-up at Vanderbilt Eye Institute (visual field test and pressure check, last month)
Cardiology check-in at Saint Thomas Heart (medication review and blood pressure management, 2 months ago)
Orthopedics/physical therapy visit for knee osteoarthritis flare (6 weeks ago)
Primary care annual physical with labs (A1c and lipid panel, 3 months ago)

finance.debt: Recently discharged from Chapter 7 bankruptcy (late 2024) after medical bills and high-interest credit card debt; currently rebuilding credit, has a secured credit card, and is on a strict budget while repaying a remaining car loan and a small IRS installment plan.
investments: 401(k) (rebuilding contributions after pause during debt crisis)
High-yield savings account (emergency fund target: 3 months)
recent_transactions: Monthly payment to IRS installment agreement (~$220)
Secured credit card deposit and first statement payment in full
Co-pay for cardiology visit and prescription refills
Used laptop replacement purchased refurbished for work-from-home days

relationships: Spouse: Priya Gupta (works part-time in medical billing; jointly managing post-bankruptcy budgeting)
Adult daughter: Anika Gupta (lives in Atlanta; provides emotional support but worries about his health)
Older sister: Sunita Patel (lives in New Jersey; occasional financial advice and family check-ins)
Neighbor/friend: Tom Reilly (walking buddy; knows limited details about the bankruptcy)
Former coworker: Denise Carter (trusted confidant, occasionally reviews his resume and job leads)
social.social_media_activity: Low-profile; uses LinkedIn to follow cybersecurity news and Nashville tech meetups, rarely posts. Keeps Facebook mostly for family photos and local community updates; privacy settings are locked down.
private_conversations: Discussing the bankruptcy timeline and what to disclose to family members
Negotiating medical bills and choosing which appointments to delay due to cost
Concerns about erectile dysfunction after cardiac event (shared only with spouse and physician)
Worry about being targeted for scams due to recent financial hardship

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri 8:30am–5:30pm with periodic after-hours incident response on a rotating on-call schedule (about one week per month).
regular_appointments: Ophthalmology check every 3–4 months for glaucoma monitoring
Physical therapy home exercise program (30 minutes, 4x/week) for osteoarthritis
Monthly budgeting session with spouse (first Sunday evening)
upcoming_events: Dental cleaning (next Tuesday, delayed previously to save money)
Vanderbilt Eye Institute appointment for OCT scan (in 3 weeks)
On-call rotation for security incidents (starts in 2 weeks)
Family visit to Atlanta to see daughter (planned for late April, dependent on finances)
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 6:15am for blood pressure check and a short walk when knees allow; remote work 2 days/week to reduce commuting costs; evenings are usually meal prep, stretching for joint pain, and reviewing bills/credit reports.

professional.company: Mid-size healthcare services provider with a security operations center (SOC) supporting clinics across Tennessee
projects: EDR rollout and tuning across endpoints (reducing false positives and improving containment playbooks)
Phishing simulation program and targeted training for high-risk departments
HIPAA-focused access review and least-privilege remediation
Incident response tabletop exercise planning with IT and compliance
SIEM rule updates for credential-stuffing and anomalous VPN access
professional.performance_issues: During the height of the debt crisis he missed a few deadlines and took extra on-call shifts for overtime, leading to fatigue and a documented coaching conversation; he has since stabilized but still worries that financial pressure could affect focus during incidents.
workplace_relationships: Manager: Karen Whitfield (supportive but expects consistent documentation and timely ticket closure)
Teammate: Jordan Kim (strong technical partner; occasionally frustrated by Michael’s cautious change-control approach)
Compliance lead: Rafael Ortega (frequent collaborator; pushes for faster audit evidence turnaround)
IT operations: Maria Sanchez (good rapport; coordinates maintenance windows to avoid outages)

hobbies: Walking at Shelby Bottoms Greenway (when joints permit)
Amateur radio (ham) and listening to emergency nets
Cooking budget-friendly Indian and Southern fusion meals at home
Watching Predators games and listening to sports radio
Volunteering occasionally at a local community center teaching basic online safety
habits: Tracks spending weekly and freezes/unfreezes credit as needed to avoid fraud
Uses a pill organizer and phone reminders for eye drops and cardiac meds
Avoids alcohol most weeks due to heart health and medication interactions
Buys generic prescriptions and uses mail-order pharmacy when cheaper
Checks credit reports monthly and disputes errors related to the bankruptcy
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with his spouse in a modest rented duplex in Donelson after downsizing from a mortgaged home during the bankruptcy; focused on rebuilding savings before considering homeownership again.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Heart-healthy, lower-sodium diet; emphasizes vegetables, lentils, fish or chicken, and limits fried foods and sugary snacks due to prediabetes and cholesterol.
